denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/01/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101243283          Group #: GRP-1157
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-125530
Claim #: CLM-2026-196322
Date of Service (requested): 02/09/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 02/11/2026 for the requested service.: Unicompartmental knee arthroplasty
Diagnosis (as submitted): [REDACTED]
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0137 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0137 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Aetna medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before Unicompartmental knee arthroplasty. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Knee osteoarthritis, severe medial compartment (M17.11).

The file does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director to review clinical information before you file a written appeal., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101243283) and reference UM-2026-125530. 
Funding: This determination applies under your employer's self-funded group arrangement (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Knee osteoarthritis, severe medial compartment (M17.11). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Unicompartmental knee arthroplasty recommended as the next appropriate step. BMI 27; failed NSAIDs, injections ×3, and 12 weeks PT; varus alignment. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that step therapy missing bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
